{{Quote|You might go to a hundred smugglers' bars and ask a hundred fellow spacers, but you will never hear a good word paid to ol' Jak Sazz. 'What's nastier inside, his mouth or his ship' is a topic that never goes away. I mean everyone's got an opinion. Really, we're always talking about this—just so he don't hear you, or you might get to find out. If you ask me, you're luckier if he eats you …|The smuggler [[Lo Khan]]|<ref name="DESB" />}}
'''Jak Sazz''' was an [[Ab'Ugartte]] who frequented the [[Byss Bistro]] on the [[Imperial Freight Complex]] above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]]. Unsociable and unhygienic, Sazz was often mocked and looked down upon by those around him. He made a living performing almost any job asked of him, including [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]], [[Slavery/Legends|slaving]], and [[Spice-jacker|spice-jacking]]. He often carried a large [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanner]] that he nicknamed "[[Grivooga]]."

Sazz considered Grivooga his only friend, carrying the tool around with him everywhere he went and having frequent conversations with it. He was known to be a good cook, although he often ate his food live. Sazz was also known for beating a crewmate who had ridiculed him to [[Death/Legends|death]] with Grivooga. Despite Sazz's unusual lifestyle, he was respected for his many abilities. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], he was present at the Byss Bistro when the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|infiltrated]] the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on the planet.

==Biography==
[[File:JakSazzDEII4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Jak Sazz often ate his food live.]]
Jak Sazz was an [[Ab'Ugartte]], a little-known [[Species/Legends|species]] not known for their congeniality, although Sazz was considered an outcast even among them. Sazz's only friend was "[[Grivooga]]," an oversized [[Hydrospanner/Legends|hydrospanner]] he constantly talked to and carried around everywhere. He typically used Grivooga to repair [[Jak Sazz's starship|his old starship]], as well as to pummel live food. Sazz frequented the [[Byss Bistro]], a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] on the [[Imperial Freight Complex]] above the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Byss/Legends|Byss]], where his bad hygiene often kept the other bar patrons as far away from him as possible.<ref name="DESB" />

Sazz was considered a lowlife by many denizens of the underworld, and even the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] were reluctant about contracting him for work. He never refused any paying job and had done work as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]], an [[Slavery/Legends|enslaver]], a [[spice-jacker]], and a [[garbage tech]] aboard a media [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]]. [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] had constantly joked that Sazz would even haul [[Deflector shield/Legends|unshielded]] [[antimatter]] for the right price until Sazz took it seriously and kept pestering Calrissian to see if he had any. A [[Jak Sazz's former crewmate|crewmate]] of Sazz's once mocked the Ab'Ugartte about his relationship with Grivooga. Sazz, not possessing much of a sense of humor, beat the crewmate to [[Death/Legends|death]] with Grivooga and cooked them for a meal. This deterred future crewmates and partners from making quips about him and Grivooga.<ref name="DESB" /> In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]],<ref name="DE5 Date">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]] to ten [[Standard year|years]] after the events of {{Film|IV}}, which equates to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> Sazz was present at the Byss Bistro after [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] resurrection in a [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] body and,<ref name="DE5">[[Dark Empire 5|''Dark Empire'' 5]]</ref> later the same [[Standard year/Legends|year]],<ref name="DEII4 Date">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' dates the events of [[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]] to ten [[Standard year|years]] after the events of {{Film|IV}}, which equates to [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> he was present there again before the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Battle of the Emperor's Citadel|attack]] on the [[Emperor's Citadel]] on Byss.<ref name="DEII4">[[Dark Empire II 4|''Dark Empire II'' 4]]</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Jak Sazz was well-known for his unhygienic lifestyle. He was never known to bathe and preferred consuming only live food. He was the subject of many jokes among his colleagues, though he had no sense of humor himself, and some argued that his continued existence was proof that entropy was winning in [[The galaxy/Legends|the universe]].<ref name="DESB" />

Despite this, many still respected Sazz's abilities. Sazz's close relationship with Grivooga was well-known, and many people made bets that he even carried the hydrospanner into the [[Turboshower/Legends|turboshower]] with him, though since there was never any evidence that Sazz had ever used a turboshower, many of these bets remained uncollected. Aside from being able to carry out almost any type of job, Sazz's other talents included cooking, [[Astrogation/Legends|astrogation]], [[Starship/Legends|starship]] repair, and archaic starship [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]].<ref name="DESB" />

==Equipment==
While in the Byss Bistro, Sazz wore a set of plated [[Armor/Legends|armor]].<ref name="DE5" /> He often carried Grivooga with him, having named the hydrospanner and formed a relationship with it.<ref name="DESB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Jak Sazz first appeared in the [[Dark Empire 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Dark Empire]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series. The issue was written by [[Tom Veitch]], illustrated by [[Cam Kennedy]], and released by [[Dark Horse Comics]]<ref name="DE5" /> on [[August 18]], [[1992]].<ref name="Marvel">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54061/star_wars_dark_empire_1991_5|text=Star Wars: Dark Empire (1991) #5}}</ref> He was first identified by name in the [[1993]] [[roleplaying game]] supplement ''[[Dark Empire Sourcebook]]'', which was authored by [[Michael Allen Horne]].<ref name="DESB" />
